visa_types.documents_required:
- The application form should be filled online at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en) 
- Please ensure to bring your  Serial number with your form. For example, Serial number:5624680 PIN: CFACS
- Make sure that either you print out the  application form yourself or save it as PDF for printout later.
- Handwritten forms will not be accepted.
- Please  take note of the following points when completing the online application.
STEP 4/6 of the online application: the Guarantor / Inviting person information is to be filled out. In case of letter of guarantee/invitation for person and business visits, applicants for tourist visas should skip this part and fill out ONLY the booked hotel/accommodation/apartment information in the following part (Information on the residence in Croatia).
STEP 5/6 of the online application: Documents upload, please upload all the documents you are going to submit in this step.
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, pleaseclick here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/hkg/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
- Applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application. Please find the documents you will need to prepare on
- Checklist  for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)
- All submitted documents must be scanned and uploaded online (step 5 of online application form filling).
Should you not attach the required documents please inform the VFS Global Visa Application staff to assist in accessing the application, scanning, and uploading the supporting documents before the submission to the Croatian Embassy in Beijing.
The applicants may find detailed information at the consular  information section of the Croatian Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs’  web site: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of  Croatia - Visas (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/22807)
The documents attached shall not be older than 3 months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification of the documents during the visa application process.

Fees are due on the visa application when
                                                                        applying.

[hrv > hkg > en > Table:ADS Group > Visa Fees 1]

The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children under the age of 12(not including 12 years’ old),
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,

visa_types.processing_time:
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa processing time is up to 10 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview:
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Since January 1, 2023, Croatia is a member of the Schengen area (Croatia issues Schengen visas valid for the Schengen area). Hong Kong passport holders do not rerequire a visa for Croatia or the Schengen area. Foreign nationals residing in Hong Kong SAR should check whether they require a visa for Croatia (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).
A visa allows foreign nationals to stay in Croatia or other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within any period of 180 days. Time spent in Croatia is added to the time spent in other Schengen countries. Short-stay Visa Calculator of permitted stay (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/border-crossing/short-stay-calculator_en) can be used as a helping tool to calculate travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa.
If the traveller plans to visit more than one Schengen country during the course of one trip, such Schengen visa application must be submitted to the embassy/consulate of the country that is the applicant’s main destination based on the length of the stay or the purpose of his/her stay. If several Schengen area countries are visited but the main destination cannot be determined, such applications must be submitted to the representative of the Schengen country that is applicant’s first point of entry into the Schengen area.
A Schengen visa contains the information on the number of times its holder is allowed to enter the Schengen area (one, two or multiple entries). If a visa is only valid for one entry and the holder of such a visa leaves the Schengen area, for example to go to the UK, Montenegro, or Bosnia and Herzegovina, re-entry to the Schengen area is not possible without a new visa. It is therefore important that applicants state in their visa application how many times they will be travelling in and out of the Schengen area during their visit.
Foreign nationals cannot work in Croatia on the basis of a short-term visa. Applicants may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Beijing. Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued. Furthermore, an issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Croatia. On every crossing of the border, a border police officer may require foreign citizens to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits.
If a visa is granted, it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Since January 1, 2023, Croatia is a member of the Schengen area (Croatia issues Schengen visas valid for the Schengen area). Hong Kong passport holders do not rerequire a visa for Croatia or the Schengen area. Foreign nationals residing in Hong Kong SAR should check whether they require a visa for Croatia (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).
A visa allows foreign nationals to stay in Croatia or other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within any period of 180 days. Time spent in Croatia is added to the time spent in other Schengen countries. Short-stay Visa Calculator of permitted stay (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/border-crossing/short-stay-calculator_en) can be used as a helping tool to calculate travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa.
If the traveller plans to visit more than one Schengen country during the course of one trip, such Schengen visa application must be submitted to the embassy/consulate of the country that is the applicant’s main destination based on the length of the stay or the purpose of his/her stay. If several Schengen area countries are visited but the main destination cannot be determined, such applications must be submitted to the representative of the Schengen country that is applicant’s first point of entry into the Schengen area.
A Schengen visa contains the information on the number of times its holder is allowed to enter the Schengen area (one, two or multiple entries). If a visa is only valid for one entry and the holder of such a visa leaves the Schengen area, for example to go to the UK, Montenegro, or Bosnia and Herzegovina, re-entry to the Schengen area is not possible without a new visa. It is therefore important that applicants state in their visa application how many times they will be travelling in and out of the Schengen area during their visit.
Foreign nationals cannot work in Croatia on the basis of a short-term visa. Applicants may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Beijing. Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued. Furthermore, an issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Croatia. On every crossing of the border, a border police officer may require foreign citizens to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits.
If a visa is granted, it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when
                                                                        applying.

[hrv > hkg > en > Table:ADS Group > Visa Fees 1]

The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children under the age of 12(not including 12 years’ old),
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations under the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa fee is 35 EUR( 306 HKD)
For further details regarding visa fee exemption please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas - Visa Requirements Overview: http://www.mvep.hr/en/consular-information/visas/visa-requirements-overview/ (http://www.mvep.hr/en/consular-information/visas/visa-requirements-overview/).

Service charge: 289 HKD

*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of VAT
- The applicable visa fee and the service fee are in Chinese yuan as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application center should pay the visa fee at the center in cash.
- Applicants exempted from the visa fee need to pay only the service fee.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.
The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Photocopying services
- Photo services
- Courier services
- SMS Tracking Facility
- Translation services

## Documents Required
- The application form should be filled online at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en) 
- Please ensure to bring your  Serial number with your form. For example, Serial number:5624680 PIN: CFACS
- Make sure that either you print out the  application form yourself or save it as PDF for printout later.
- Handwritten forms will not be accepted.
- Please  take note of the following points when completing the online application.
STEP 4/6 of the online application: the Guarantor / Inviting person information is to be filled out. In case of letter of guarantee/invitation for person and business visits, applicants for tourist visas should skip this part and fill out ONLY the booked hotel/accommodation/apartment information in the following part (Information on the residence in Croatia).
STEP 5/6 of the online application: Documents upload, please upload all the documents you are going to submit in this step.
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, pleaseclick here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/hkg/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
- Applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application. Please find the documents you will need to prepare on
- Checklist  for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)
- All submitted documents must be scanned and uploaded online (step 5 of online application form filling).
Should you not attach the required documents please inform the VFS Global Visa Application staff to assist in accessing the application, scanning, and uploading the supporting documents before the submission to the Croatian Embassy in Beijing.
The applicants may find detailed information at the consular  information section of the Croatian Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs’  web site: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of  Croatia - Visas (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/22807)
The documents attached shall not be older than 3 months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

                                                                    
- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Color photograph printed on normal photographic paper.
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm.
- 70-80% of the photograph should be covered by face.
- Full face straight profile photograph,
                                                                            30-36 mm from point of the chin to head.
- Should be taken against a light background.
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap.
- Photographs can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case-to-case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes.
- The mouth must be closed.
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications.
To ensure your visa application complies with biometric standards, digitally altered photographs are strictly prohibited for submission.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa processing time is up to 10 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview:
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Visa application form must be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer. The visa application form must be filled out in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form must be filled out electronically (online application form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)) and then submitted to the VFS with the on-line application serial number
- Please ensure to bring your Serial number with your form.
- For example, Serial number: 5624680 PIN: CGACS

Handwritten forms will not be accepted,
- Guarantee  letter - Natural Person can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3YuIVualSxFGPbIteoUQdR/fb559c45a339dc795b672912d262e2c0/01-letter-of-guarantee-natural-person-JP-FO-1.11.pdf)
- Guarantee  letter – Legal Person can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/77dVdqQhbPSLnkqFIV7kv9/749b80a94b17097267ca90d03219df00/02-letter-of-guarantee_-legal-person-JP-PO-1.11.pdf)
- Checklist for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when
                                                                        applying.

[hrv > hkg > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon
                                                                        application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse
                                                                            and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member
                                                                            States,
- Children under the age of 12(not including
                                                                            12 years’ old),
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports
                                                                            travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and
                                                                            teachers accompanying them when they come to
                                                                            attend school, study and professional
                                                                            training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as
                                                                            provided for by the Recommendation of the
                                                                            European Parliament and of the Council of 28
                                                                            September 2005 to facilitate the issue by
                                                                            the Member
                                                                            States of uniform short-stay visas for
                                                                            researchers from third countries travelling
                                                                            within the Community for the purpose of
                                                                            carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations
                                                                            under the age of 25, who are to attend
                                                                            seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or
                                                                            educational events organized by non-profit
                                                                            organizations.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa fee is 35 EUR(  298 HKD)
For further details regarding visa fee exemption
                                                                        please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements
                                                                        available on the website of the Ministry of
                                                                        Foreign and European Affairs under Consular
                                                                        Information – Visas - Visa Requirements
                                                                        Overview: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).
                                                                    
Service charge: 289 HKD
- The applicable visa fee and the service fee
                                                                            are in Hong Kong dollar as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application center should pay the visa fee at the center in cash.
- Applicants exempted from the visa fee need
                                                                            to pay only the service fee.
- The fees are non-refundable and
                                                                            non-transferable.

                                                                        services at a nominal additional cost: 
- Photocopying services
- Photo services
- Courier services within HK
- Courier services in Macau and China
- SMS Tracking Facility

## Documents Required
- The application form should be filled online at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en) 
- Please ensure to bring your  Serial number with your form. For example, Serial number:5624680 PIN: CFACS
- Make sure that either you print out the  application form yourself or save it as PDF for printout later.
- Handwritten forms will not be accepted.
- Please  take note of the following points when completing the online application.
STEP 4/6 of the online application: the Guarantor / Inviting person information is to be filled out. In case of letter of guarantee/invitation for person and business visits, applicants for tourist visas should skip this part and fill out ONLY the booked hotel/accommodation/apartment information in the following part (Information on the residence in Croatia).
STEP 5/6 of the online application: Documents upload, please upload all the documents you are going to submit in this step.
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, pleaseclick here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/hkg/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
- Applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application. Please find the documents you will need to prepare on
- Checklist  for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)
- All submitted documents must be scanned and uploaded online (step 5 of online application form filling).
Should you not attach the required documents please inform the VFS Global Visa Application staff to assist in accessing the application, scanning, and uploading the supporting documents before the submission to the Croatian Embassy in Beijing.
The applicants may find detailed information at the consular  information section of the Croatian Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs’  web site: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of  Croatia - Visas (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/22807)
The documents attached shall not be older than 3 months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

                                                                    
- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Color photograph printed on normal photographic paper.
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm.
- 70-80% of the photograph should be covered by face.
- Full face straight profile photograph,
                                                                            30-36 mm from point of the chin to head.
- Should be taken against a light background.
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap.
- Photographs can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case-to-case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes.
- The mouth must be closed.
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications.
To ensure your visa application complies with biometric standards, digitally altered photographs are strictly prohibited for submission.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa processing time is up to 10 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview:
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Visa application form must be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer. The visa application form must be filled out in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form must be filled out electronically (online application form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)) and then submitted to the VFS with the on-line application serial number
- Please ensure to bring your Serial number with your form.
- For example, Serial number: 5624680 PIN: CGACS

Handwritten forms will not be accepted,
- Guarantee  letter - Natural Person can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3YuIVualSxFGPbIteoUQdR/fb559c45a339dc795b672912d262e2c0/01-letter-of-guarantee-natural-person-JP-FO-1.11.pdf)
- Guarantee  letter – Legal Person can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/77dVdqQhbPSLnkqFIV7kv9/749b80a94b17097267ca90d03219df00/02-letter-of-guarantee_-legal-person-JP-PO-1.11.pdf)
- Checklist for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Since January 1, 2023, Croatia is a member of the Schengen area (Croatia issues Schengen visas valid for the Schengen area). Hong Kong passport holders do not rerequire a visa for Croatia or the Schengen area. Foreign nationals residing in Hong Kong SAR should check whether they require a visa for Croatia (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).
A visa allows foreign nationals to stay in Croatia or other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within any period of 180 days. Time spent in Croatia is added to the time spent in other Schengen countries. Short-stay Visa Calculator of permitted stay (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/border-crossing/short-stay-calculator_en) can be used as a helping tool to calculate travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa.
If the traveller plans to visit more than one Schengen country during the course of one trip, such Schengen visa application must be submitted to the embassy/consulate of the country that is the applicant’s main destination based on the length of the stay or the purpose of his/her stay. If several Schengen area countries are visited but the main destination cannot be determined, such applications must be submitted to the representative of the Schengen country that is applicant’s first point of entry into the Schengen area.
A Schengen visa contains the information on the number of times its holder is allowed to enter the Schengen area (one, two or multiple entries). If a visa is only valid for one entry and the holder of such a visa leaves the Schengen area, for example to go to the UK, Montenegro, or Bosnia and Herzegovina, re-entry to the Schengen area is not possible without a new visa. It is therefore important that applicants state in their visa application how many times they will be travelling in and out of the Schengen area during their visit.
Foreign nationals cannot work in Croatia on the basis of a short-term visa. Applicants may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Beijing. Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued. Furthermore, an issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Croatia. On every crossing of the border, a border police officer may require foreign citizens to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits.
If a visa is granted, it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.

## Overview
Since January 1, 2023, Croatia is a member of the Schengen area (Croatia issues Schengen visas valid for the Schengen area). A visa allows foreign nationals to stay in Croatia or other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within any period of 180 days. Time spent in Croatia is added to the time spent in other Schengen countries. Short-stay Visa Calculator of permitted stay (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/border-crossing/short-stay-calculator_en) can be used as a helping tool to calculate travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa.
If the traveller plans to visit more than one Schengen country during the course of one trip, such Schengen visa application must be submitted to the embassy/consulate of the country that is the applicant’s main destination based on the length of the stay or the purpose of his/her stay. If several Schengen area countries are visited but the main destination cannot be determined, such applications must be submitted to the representative of the Schengen country that is applicant’s first point of entry into the Schengen area.
A Schengen visa contains the information on the number of times its holder is allowed to enter the Schengen area (one, two or multiple entries). If a visa is only valid for one entry and the holder of such a visa leaves the Schengen area, for example to go to the UK, Montenegro, or Bosnia and Herzegovina, re-entry to the Schengen area is not possible without a new visa. It is therefore important that applicants state in their visa application how many times they will be travelling in and out of the Schengen area during their visit.
Foreign nationals cannot work in Croatia on the basis of a short-term visa. Applicants may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Beijing. Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued. Furthermore, an issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Croatia. On every crossing of the border, a border police officer may require foreign citizens to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits.
If a visa is granted, it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when
                                                                        applying.

[hrv > hkg > en > Table:Family or
                                                                        Friends Visit > Visa Fees 1]

- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children under the age of 12(not including 12 years’ old),
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students, and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations under the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa fee is 35 EUR( 306 HKD)

For further details regarding visa fee exemption please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas - Visa Requirements Overview: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).

- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

- Photocopying services
- Photo services

## Documents Required
- The application form should be filled online at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en) 
- Please ensure to bring your  Serial number with your form. For example, Serial number:5624680 PIN: CFACS
- Make sure that either you print out the  application form yourself or save it as PDF for printout later.

In case of letter of guarantee/invitation for person and business visits, applicants for tourist visas should skip this part and fill out ONLY the booked hotel/accommodation/apartment information in the following part (Information on the residence in Croatia).

- Checklist  for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)
- All submitted documents must be scanned and uploaded online (step 5 of online application form filling).
Should you not attach the required documents please inform the VFS Global Visa Application staff to assist in accessing the application, scanning, and uploading the supporting documents before the submission to the Croatian Embassy in Beijing.

The documents attached shall not be older than 3 months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

                                                                    
- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Color photograph printed on normal photographic paper.
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm.
- 70-80% of the photograph should be covered by face.
- Full face straight profile photograph,
                                                                            30-36 mm from point of the chin to head.
- Should be taken against a light background.
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap.
- Photographs can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case-to-case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes.
- The mouth must be closed.
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications.
To ensure your visa application complies with biometric standards, digitally altered photographs are strictly prohibited for submission.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa processing time is up to 10 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy. Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when
                                                                        applying.

[hrv > hkg > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children under the age of 12(not including 12 years’ old),
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students, and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional training,
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations under the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine visa fee is 35 EUR( 306 HKD)For further details regarding visa fee exemption please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas - Visa Requirements Overview: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).

Service charge: 289 HKD
- The applicable visa fee and the service fee are in Hong Kong Dollar as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application center should pay the visa fee at the center in cash.
- Applicants exempted from the visa fee need to pay only the service fee.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.
The Visa Application Centre offers the following services at a nominal additional cost:
- Photocopying services
- Photo services
- Courier services within HK
- Courier services in China and Macau.
- SMS Tracking Facility

## Documents Required
- The application form should be filled online at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en) 
- Please ensure to bring your  Serial number with your form. For example, Serial number:5624680 PIN: CFACS
- Make sure that either you print out the  application form yourself or save it as PDF for printout later.
- Handwritten forms will not be accepted.
- Please  take note of the following points when completing the online application.
STEP 4/6 of the online application: the Guarantor / Inviting person information is to be filled out. In case of letter of guarantee/invitation for person and business visits, applicants for tourist visas should skip this part and fill out ONLY the booked hotel/accommodation/apartment information in the following part (Information on the residence in Croatia).
STEP 5/6 of the online application: Documents upload, please upload all the documents you are going to submit in this step.
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, pleaseclick here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/hkg/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
- Applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application. Please find the documents you will need to prepare on
- Checklist  for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)
- All submitted documents must be scanned and uploaded online (step 5 of online application form filling).
Should you not attach the required documents please inform the VFS Global Visa Application staff to assist in accessing the application, scanning, and uploading the supporting documents before the submission to the Croatian Embassy in Beijing.
The applicants may find detailed information at the consular  information section of the Croatian Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs’  web site: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of  Croatia - Visas (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/22807)
The documents attached shall not be older than 3 months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

                                                                    
- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Color photograph printed on normal photographic paper.
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm.
- 70-80% of the photograph should be covered by face.
- Full face straight profile photograph,
                                                                            30-36 mm from point of the chin to head.
- Should be taken against a light background.
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap.
- Photographs can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case-to-case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes.
- The mouth must be closed.
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications.
To ensure your visa application complies with biometric standards, digitally altered photographs are strictly prohibited for submission.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa processing time is up to 10 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview:
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Visa application form must be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer. The visa application form must be filled out in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form must be filled out electronically (online application form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)) and then submitted to the VFS with the on-line application serial number
- Please ensure to bring your Serial number with your form.
- For example, Serial number: 5624680 PIN: CGACS

Handwritten forms will not be accepted,
- Guarantee  letter - Natural Person can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3YuIVualSxFGPbIteoUQdR/fb559c45a339dc795b672912d262e2c0/01-letter-of-guarantee-natural-person-JP-FO-1.11.pdf)
- Guarantee  letter – Legal Person can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/77dVdqQhbPSLnkqFIV7kv9/749b80a94b17097267ca90d03219df00/02-letter-of-guarantee_-legal-person-JP-PO-1.11.pdf)
- Checklist for Croatia Schengen Visa can be downloaded from here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/e63Oei3Jxn4uioETAk0Kz/275e21c0af321641029a1728479505db/HONG-KONG-SAR-C-VISA-ENG-CHECK-LIST-2025.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Since January 1, 2023, Croatia is a member of the Schengen area (Croatia issues Schengen visas valid for the Schengen area). Hong Kong passport holders do not rerequire a visa for Croatia or the Schengen area. Foreign nationals residing in Hong Kong SAR should check whether they require a visa for Croatia (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).
A visa allows foreign nationals to stay in Croatia or other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within any period of 180 days. Time spent in Croatia is added to the time spent in other Schengen countries. Short-stay Visa Calculator of permitted stay (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/border-crossing/short-stay-calculator_en) can be used as a helping tool to calculate travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa.
If the traveller plans to visit more than one Schengen country during the course of one trip, such Schengen visa application must be submitted to the embassy/consulate of the country that is the applicant’s main destination based on the length of the stay or the purpose of his/her stay. If several Schengen area countries are visited but the main destination cannot be determined, such applications must be submitted to the representative of the Schengen country that is applicant’s first point of entry into the Schengen area.
A Schengen visa contains the information on the number of times its holder is allowed to enter the Schengen area (one, two or multiple entries). If a visa is only valid for one entry and the holder of such a visa leaves the Schengen area, for example to go to the UK, Montenegro, or Bosnia and Herzegovina, re-entry to the Schengen area is not possible without a new visa. It is therefore important that applicants state in their visa application how many times they will be travelling in and out of the Schengen area during their visit.
Foreign nationals cannot work in Croatia on the basis of a short-term visa. Applicants may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Beijing. Submitting a visa application, including all the required documents, does not automatically mean that the visa will be issued. Furthermore, an issued visa is not a guarantee that a foreign citizen will be permitted to enter the Republic of Croatia. On every crossing of the border, a border police officer may require foreign citizens to present all relevant documents, including copies of the Letters of Guarantee for both private and business visits.
If a visa is granted, it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
Any falsification of the documents and the use of such documents may be subject to criminal prosecution.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

Visa type D substitutes the procedure of short-term (type C) visa applications of third-country nationals who have been granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit by the competent Police Directorate/Police Administration.
The long-term visa is an approval to stay in the territory of the Republic of Croatia up to 30 days if a third-country national has been granted temporary stay for:
- family reunification,
- secondary school education,
- university studies,
- research,
- humanitarian grounds,
- life partnership,
- work,
- work of a posted worker,
- stay of long-term residents in another EEA Member State,
- stay of digital nomads,
- other purposes
Visa type D is issued:
- to third-country nationals who have been granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit, and are required to hold a short-term visa to enter the Republic of Croatia;
- for maximum 30-days' stay;
- for one (1) or multiple (MULT) entries;
- with the period of validity no longer than six months; long-term visa shall not become valid prior to the beginning of validity of the granted temporary stay or the issued stay and work permit in the Republic of Croatia; in a valid travel document in the form of the sticker also used for the issuance of visas type C; the letter “D” is entered into the category “Type of visa”.
The procedure of issuance of long-term visas is identical to the procedure employed until now for the issuance of short-term visas to third-country nationals who have been granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit by the competent Police Directorate/Police Administration.A long-term visa application shall be considered admissible if:it was submitted no sooner than two months before and no later than two months after the beginning of validity of temporary stay or the issued stay and work permit in the Republic of Croatiait was submitted on a prescribed forma travel document is presented along with the application with validity at least three months longer than the validity of the granted temporary stay it includes a photographthe third-country national has been granted temporary stay or issued a stay and work permit in the Republic of Croatiathe prescribed administrative fee has been paid ( HKD 793)If the above conditions are not fulfilled, long-term visa application shall be considered inadmissible, all documents enclosed shall be returned to the applicant, and the amount of administrative fee paid shall be refunded.When applying for a long-term visa, the applicant is obliged to submit:
- filled in and personally signed prescribed long-term visa (type D) application form;
- valid travel document with at least two empty pages, issued within the past ten years, with validity at least three months longer than the validity of the granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit;
- a photograph of identical characteristics to the one submitted with the application for short-term visas;
- proof of granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit in the Republic of Croatia;
- proof of means of transportation;
- proof of travel health insurance, if applicable;
- proof of payment of the administrative fee ( HKD 793).

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when
                                                                        applying.

The following are exempt from visa fees upon
                                                                        application:
- Children up to 12 years of age
- Family reunification
- Secondary school education
- University studies
- Research
- Humanitarian grounds
For further details regarding visa fee exemption please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas - Visa Requirements Overview: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879).
Service charge: 289 HKD
Note:
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application center should pay the visa fee at the center in cash.
- Applicants exempted from the visa fee need to pay only VFS Service fee and Logistic fee.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

When applying for a long-term visa, the applicant is obliged to submit:
Please see the checklist for D type applications
- On-Line Visa Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
 
duly completed, signed by the applicant in person at the end of the application form.
- One recent photograph in color, 35x45 mm, glued on the application form.
- Passport issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least three months longer than the validity of the granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit.
- Copies of the main page of applicant's passport. Copies of Schengen visas, if issued
- Proof of granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit in the Republic of Croatia (copy acceptable)
- Proof of means of transportation: copy of the one-way travel ticket reservation
- Medical travel insurance should be:

- valid for Croatia or world-wide
- valid for 30 days counting from the date of entering in to Croatia containing a minimum coverage of 30.000 EUR for urgent medical treatment and repatriation in case of death
- For Third Country Nationals not residing in their domicile country:

copy of residence permit
- For minors, under 18:

copy of the birth certificate
- Other possible documents considered necessary by the applicant.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

                                                                    
- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Color photograph printed on normal photographic paper.
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm.
- 70-80% of the photograph should be covered by face.
- Full face straight profile photograph,
                                                                            30-36 mm from point of the chin to head.
- Should be taken against a light background.
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap.
- Photographs can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case-to-case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes.
- The mouth must be closed.
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications.
To ensure your visa application complies with biometric standards, digitally altered photographs are strictly prohibited for submission.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports) and Ukraine, visa processing time is up to 10 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy, except the delivery time to and from satellite city ( Hong Kong) to and from Embassy. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview:
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Croatia - Visa requirements overview (gov.hr) (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Visa application form must be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer. The visa application form must be filled out in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form must be filled out electronically (online application form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)) and then submitted to the VFS with the on-line application serial number
